title: Fatigue Characterization of EEG under Mixed Reality Stereo Vision
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 24 electrodes acquired during a mixed reality stereo vision study designed to characterize mental fatigue. Participants completed a protocol involving resting scenes and movement observation tasks with periodic reciprocal movements. The dataset supports both resting-state and task-state EEG analysis, providing a resource for investigating fatigue-related neural correlates under immersive visual conditions.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 24 electrodes positioned across frontal (Fp1, Fp2, AF3, AF4, F7, Fz, F8, FC5, FC6), temporal (FT7, FT8), central (C3, Cz, C4, CP3, CP4), parietal (P3, Pz, P4, PO3, PO4), and occipital (O1, Oz, O2) regions. Participants completed a protocol consisting of 2 resting scenes and 15 movement scenes, each movement scene containing 20 trials of reciprocal periodic movements at fixed depth and velocity, followed by rating scenes.

funding_references.funder_name: Jilin Scientific and Technology Development Program
